hard.next_step: June 16 2026 - Wendy: Continue market review over the next 12-18 months and reach out to Edward directly if you'd like to explore a deeper discussion or tailored demo
hard.ais_why: Wendy Rocco sat through the March 2026 demo of Merlin Intake, autonomous tail-spend negotiation, and AI supplier onboarding, but cut Edward off mid-pitch to focus on core functionality, not the AI story. Her takeaway was to continue a 12-18 month market review with no urgency (Jaggaer has 18 months left). Polite curiosity, demo absorbed, low conversion signal.

north_star_verdict.headline: Early qualified deal running on the buyer's own long clock: Wendy Rocco explored the market on the 6 Mar intro call but set a 12-18 month review with no budget and no urgency, and has not answered Edward's 16 Jun nurture, so the December close is a placeholder against a Jaggaer contract that runs to roughly September 2027.
north_star_verdict.math: 152 days to close (2026-12-05) from 2026-07-06. Created 2026-03-09, in Qualified stage for 119 days. Only buyer conversation is the 2026-03-06 intro call (122 days ago); no buyer-facing meeting since. Amount rose 110k to 350k on 2026-03-17 (rep-side scoping, not a buyer commitment). Rep nurture on 2026-06-16 and a Horizon 2026 invite logged 2026-06-24 are rep-side touches with no buyer reply. Buyer stated a 12-18 month timeline and an ~18-month Jaggaer runway from March 2026 (to ~Sept 2027), so the 2026-12-05 close date cannot hold and the real decision window is mid-2027 at earliest.
evidence: Wendy Rocco on the 2026-03-06 intro call: 'We're still under contract for, like, another year and a half. So right now is the time that I really wanna start just exploring what else is out there.'
Zycus rep Edward Dlugosz on 2026-03-06 acknowledged there is no active deal: 'right now, there really isn't a budget or a project or even maybe a need,' and Wendy replied 'Correct.'

Next Step 2026-06-16 is rep-initiated nurture, not buyer engagement: 'Continue market review over the next 12-18 months and reach out to Edward directly if you'd like to explore a deeper discussion or tailored demo.'
Last logged activity 2026-06-24 is an outbound Horizon 2026 conference invitation (Call Attempted), a rep touch with no buyer response; no follow-up call has occurred since the March intro.
The 2026-12-05 close date is inconsistent with the buyer's stated 12-18 month review window and the ~September 2027 Jaggaer contract expiry, so the forecast date is a placeholder.

metrics.narrative: Wendy Rocco (Director of Procurement: Indirect) named real operational pain on the 2026-03-06 intro call but quantified none of it. She confirmed end users bypass the current Jaggaer system, driving maverick spend and loss of contracted pricing, and described a lean indirect team fielding manual NDA and support requests ('Typically, they would go to someone on my team, or they would go through legal'). When Edward asked 'do you know how big your tail spend is?' she answered only 'I have an idea' and gave no figure. No dollar leakage, cycle-time, headcount cost, or savings target has been stated, so there is no ROI case yet. Building a quantified savings model with Wendy is the move that would convert this qualitative pain into a reason to act before the Jaggaer renewal.
sources: Avoma intro call 2026-03-06, Wendy Rocco: end users bypass system causing maverick spend and loss of contracted pricing
Avoma intro call 2026-03-06, Wendy Rocco: 'I have an idea' (tail-spend size, unquantified)
SF AIS_Why__c: demo absorbed, low conversion signal

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is engaged or named in Salesforce; Wendy Rocco is the only contact role and she explicitly confirmed there is no budget or project ('there really isn't a budget or a project or even maybe a need' - Edward; 'Correct.' - Wendy, 2026-03-06). The conversation does point to where budget authority sits: Wendy said 'I report up to the VP who manages the direct side of the business,' so that direct-side VP (unnamed in Salesforce) is the likely budget owner for any S2P purchase, above Wendy's indirect remit. Because that person is not on any contact role, task, or call, they cannot be named here. Mapping and meeting the direct-side VP Wendy reports to is the ranked move that would establish real budget authority.

decision_criteria.narrative: Wendy signalled the criteria she cares about even though no formal RFI or scorecard exists yet. On 2026-03-06 she steered away from the AI pitch to core functionality ('I would be happy with a list of your functionality... functionally, what does your tool do?'), probed market fit for a ~$1.3B company ('are you only a high end market solution?'), asked whether modules can be right-sized ('is there a way to right size the need, or is it all in one package?'), and asked about support models. Her evaluation frame is functional parity with Jaggaer plus right-sizing and fit, not the agentic-AI vision. These are inferred-from-conversation criteria, not a documented decision framework; a formal criteria set will only exist once she runs a real evaluation.
sources: Avoma intro call 2026-03-06, Wendy Rocco: 'I would be happy with a list of your functionality'

decision_process.narrative: There is no buyer decision process yet; this is solo, unbudgeted exploration. Only one person (Wendy Rocco) has appeared on any call, she set the next step as self-directed ('reach out to Edward directly if you'd like to explore'), and she framed the whole exercise as a 12-18 month market review timed to the Jaggaer renewal, not a live procurement cycle. No approval chain, committee, or evaluation stages have been described. The process will only take shape when Wendy pulls in her direct-side VP and any IT or finance stakeholders; until then Zycus is single-threaded to one director with no path to a decision defined.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are in view because the deal is pre-budget and pre-evaluation. No MSA, Order Form, SOW, InfoSec review, or procurement channel has been discussed, and no partner or SI is involved. The one forcing function on record is the incumbent Jaggaer contract, which Wendy said has roughly 18 months left from March 2026 (to about September 2027); that expiry, not the 2026-12-05 close date, is the real clock. Visibility into any paper process will only come once a genuine evaluation and budget exist.
sources: Avoma intro call 2026-03-06, Wendy Rocco: 'We're still under contract for, like, another year and a half'

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is clearly articulated and owned by Wendy Rocco (Director of Procurement: Indirect). On 2026-03-06 she confirmed end users bypass the current system, causing maverick spend and loss of contracted pricing, and described her lean indirect team absorbing manual work (NDAs routed through Jaggaer and DocuSign, end users escalating to her team or legal). Edward reinforced the adoption problem ('90% of procurement software rollouts fail to expectations due largely to low user adoption') and she engaged with the Merlin Intake fix. The pain is real and structural, but note it is Wendy's operational pain, not yet an executive-level compelling event: she has an 18-month runway and stated no urgency, so the pain is confirmed but not pressing.
sources: Avoma intro call 2026-03-06, Wendy Rocco: end users bypass system causing maverick spend and loss of contracted pricing
Avoma intro call 2026-03-06, Wendy Rocco on manual NDA handling: 'Typically, they would go to someone on my team, or they would go through legal'
Avoma intro call 2026-03-06 note: 90% of rollouts fail due to low user adoption / complexity

champion.narrative: Wendy Rocco (Director of Procurement: Indirect) is a developing, currently passive champion, not yet an active one. She initiated the relationship, engaged sharply for the full intro call, owns the indirect procurement function and its pain, and said re-contact is 'probably more than likely' after her market scan. What is still missing is decisive: she has demonstrated no access to the economic buyer (the direct-side VP she reports to), offered no introductions, requested no next meeting, and has not responded to Edward's 16 Jun nurture (122 days of silence since the only call). She also deflected the AI story to focus on core functionality, so she is not yet advocating a Zycus vision internally. She is a well-qualified coach with real category ownership, but her advocacy and reach into power remain unproven.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: Wendy Rocco (Director of Procurement: Indirect), primary and only contact
Avoma intro call 2026-03-06, Wendy Rocco: 'if in the event I need to get back on in touch with you, which is probably more than likely'
Next Step 2026-06-16 rep soft-touch with no buyer reply; no re-initiation since 2026-03-06

competition.narrative: Jaggaer is the incumbent and the only named rival; the more likely deal-killer is inertia, not another vendor. Wendy confirmed the incumbent on 2026-03-06 ('You're probably using Jagger' -> 'Yeah. I think I told Rishab that') and that it has roughly 18 months left. The competitive wedge is clear and was landed on the call: Merlin Intake, the MS Teams conversational chatbot that Edward said 'Jagger doesn't even have,' plus agentic AI for touchless invoicing, autonomous tail-spend negotiation, and automated supplier onboarding. But with no budget, no urgency, and a comfortable 18-month runway, the real threat is do-nothing: Wendy keeps Jaggaer and renews unless Zycus builds a quantified reason to switch. No other vendor (Coupa, Ariba, GEP) has been named; the open risk is that her long market scan invites them in before she re-engages.

competitive_position.summary: Jaggaer is the incumbent to displace and the only named competitor, but the single strongest CURRENT threat is do-nothing: Wendy Rocco has ~18 months left on Jaggaer, said there is no budget or need, and is running a 12-18 month market review, so the base case is that Associated Materials simply renews Jaggaer and never signs. The Zycus wedge (Merlin Intake MS Teams chatbot, which the rep said 'Jagger doesn't even have,' plus agentic tail-spend negotiation and supplier onboarding) is real and was landed on the 6 Mar call, but it has not created urgency. No Coupa, Ariba, GEP, or Ivalua has been named; the watch item is that a long open market scan could invite one in before Zycus re-engages.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: This is an unbudgeted, single-threaded market-scan deal: Wendy Rocco liked the Merlin Intake wedge on 6 Mar but has ~18 months left on Jaggaer, told us there is no budget or need, and set her own 12-18 month clock, so the real contest is switch-now versus renew-Jaggaer and she is not answering our nudges.

win_position.text: Win position is low: Wendy Rocco confirmed there is 'no budget or project or even maybe a need' (6 Mar) and is running a 12-18 month market review, so the base case is she renews Jaggaer rather than buys.
The one positive is a clean wedge she engaged with: Edward showed Merlin Intake in MS Teams, which he said 'Jagger doesn't even have,' against her stated maverick-spend and low-adoption pain (6 Mar).
No economic buyer is engaged: Wendy owns only indirect procurement and reports to an unnamed direct-side VP who holds the budget, and she has offered no introduction (6 Mar).
The value case is unquantified: asked how big her tail spend is she said only 'I have an idea' (6 Mar), so there is no ROI reason to switch mid-contract.

deal_momentum.text: The only two-way buyer engagement is the 6 Mar intro call, 122 days ago; there has been no buyer-facing meeting since.
Edward's 16 Jun nurture and the 24 Jun Horizon 2026 invitation are both rep-side touches with no buyer reply, so momentum is flat and rep-driven.
The 110k to 350k amount change on 17 Mar was rep-side scoping by Edward Dlugosz, not a buyer commitment, so it does not signal real progress.

customer_commitment.text: Wendy put the onus on herself to re-initiate ('reach out to Edward directly if you'd like to explore') and has not, showing passive rather than committed engagement.
She deflected the agentic-AI story to focus on a functionality list and deferred any decision 12-18 months, absorbing the demo without converting.
She requested no next meeting, gave no dates, and named no other stakeholders, so commitment sits at one curious director with no buying signal.

deal_risk.text: Do-nothing is the primary risk: an ~18-month Jaggaer runway plus no budget means the likely outcome is renewal, a no-decision loss, not a competitive one.
Single-threading to Wendy is a real risk; if she disengages or leaves, Zycus has no other relationship at Associated Materials.
The open 12-18 month market scan could pull in Coupa, Ariba, or GEP before Zycus re-engages, eroding the current first-mover position.
